BUYER: Reduce latency by 30%? That sounds… optimistic. We've got our own internal systems for that, and frankly, they're not exactly bleeding edge, but they work. And right now, "optimistic" isn't what I need. I need to fix this deployment rollback that's gone sideways.

SELLER: I appreciate your candidness, Alex. And I certainly don't want to add to your current firefighting. We essentially provide a layer that can identify bottlenecks and potential data corruption *before* it impacts your live environments, even during rapid feature rollouts.

BUYER: Look, Sarah, I'm sure your solution has its merits, but the timing is just terrible. We're deep in a major platform rewrite, and the last thing we need is to introduce another complex piece of infrastructure, no matter how "seamlessly" it's built. We're already juggling integration challenges with our existing stack. Plus, frankly, data processing isn't our top priority this quarter. It's stabilizing the core application.

SELLER: I hear you, Alex. A platform rewrite is a significant undertaking. It’s about future-proofing and reducing technical debt. We’ve seen it save engineering teams hundreds of hours in debugging and rework down the line.

BUYER: I understand the theory, but I have to be realistic about what my team can handle right now. We’re stretched thin, and the immediate problems need solving first. Implementing a new data processing solution, even if it's good, is a significant investment of time and resources for my team that we just don't have. I'm going to have to decline this conversation for now.

SELLER: Hi Alex, this is Sarah from DeepData AI. I know your time is valuable, so I'll be brief. The reason I mention it is that many of our clients who use robust databases like yours are finding that the *processing* layer, especially for advanced AI initiatives, can become a bottleneck. DeepData AI integrates seamlessly with PostgreSQL-compatible databases, including CockroachDB, to accelerate that processing without requiring a full re-architecture of your core systems.

BUYER: "Seamlessly" is a strong word. We've had issues in the past with new tools integrating cleanly with our existing stack.
